success_story.description: Nandita Lakshmanan is the Founder and CEO of The Practice, one of India's leading independent public relations firms. She began her career at Genesis PR in 1993 and played a major role in developing technology-focused PR operations. She has worked with global organizations such as Intel, IBM Software Group, 3M, Volvo, Symantec, and Varity Perkins.

ai_insights.why_choose: Public Relations is an excellent career for individuals who enjoy communication, networking, storytelling, brand building, and reputation management. It combines strategic thinking with creative communication.

career.name: Actuary
career.description: Actuaries are professionals who assess and manage financial risks associated with uncertain future events. They use mathematics, statistics, data analytics, financial modeling, and data science techniques to evaluate risks, forecast future trends, and support decision-making in insurance, finance, pensions, and investment sectors.
personality_traits: Maintains accurate records of expenses and income

educational_pathway.description: Complete 10+2 in Commerce or Science stream with Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics.
Qualify the Actuarial Common Entrance Test (ACET).
Become a member of the Institute of Actuaries of India (IAI).
Clear Core Principles examinations and practical requirements to become an Associate Actuary.
Start working as an Associate Actuary.
After becoming an Associate, clear Specialist Principles and Specialist Advanced examinations to become a Fellow Actuary.
Pursue a Bachelor's degree in Actuarial Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Engineering, or a related field before qualifying ACET and completing actuarial certification requirements.
Pursue a Bachelor's degree followed by a Master's degree in Actuarial Science or a related field before completing actuarial certification requirements.

education.note: Please check the duration of the course and actuarial examination requirements during enrolment.

course_fee.note: These figures are estimated and may vary from institute to institute.

salary.note: Figures are indicative and subject to change. Experienced actuaries may earn significantly more.

example_from_field.name: Deepak Kumar
example_from_field.details: Deepak Kumar comes from a small town in India and dreamt of becoming a doctor. He could not clear the medical entrance exams despite multiple attempts and had almost lost hope of becoming a healthcare professional. Then he heard about Emergency Medical Technician training and decided to pursue a six months Emergency Medical Technician (EMT) training program from VIVO Healthcare. Deepak completed his training and got well versed with all the necessary skills required for an Emergency Medical Technician (EMT). Deepak's doors for healthcare widened and very soon he got campus placement in one of the leading healthcare companies in India.

example_from_field.name: Pinky Kumari
example_from_field.hometown: Village of Gobrahia, West Champaran District
example_from_field.details: Pinky Kumari is a 21-year-old girl from the village of Gobrahia in the West Champaran district. It wasn't all smooth sailing for Pinky as she is from a financially poor background and had to fight against all odds. She lives with her parents, three sisters and one brother. In order to let her siblings continue their education, she had to drop school after her Class 12 as she wanted to support her family financially. Pinky then opted for the General Duty Assistant Course which is supported by HSBC. Pinky's confidence secured her a job as a Nursing Assistant in Hyderabad where her present salary is more than INR 16,000 with accommodation. Pinky wishes to become a good nurse someday.

career_journey: Production Engineer at Calcom Electronics
Worked at ICL India
Worked at Churchgate Group, Nigeria
System Marketing Manager at NSN India (1995)
Senior Vice President, Nokia Networks Asia Pacific
Chief Executive Officer of Nokia Solutions and Networks
example_from_field.details: Born in 1967, Rajiv Suri is an Indian-born Singaporean business executive. He graduated as an Electronics and Telecommunications Engineer from Mangalore University in 1989. He started his professional life as a Production Engineer at Calcom Electronics. After spending five years in ICL India and Churchgate Group, Nigeria, he moved to NSN India as System Marketing Manager in 1995. Within a decade he rose to the position of Senior Vice President Nokia Networks Asia Pacific and on October 1, 2009 he was appointed Chief Executive Officer of Nokia Solutions and Networks (then Nokia Siemens Networks).

example_from_field.name: Chandrani Prasad Verma
example_from_field.achievement: India's First Female Mining Engineer
example_from_field.organization: Central Institute of Mining and Fuel Research, Nagpur
example_from_field.details: Chandrani Prasad Verma is known across the country as its first female mining engineer. She initially faced challenges gaining admission to a diploma programme in mining because the management was reluctant to offer a seat to a woman. She later fought a court case to gain admission into a B.Tech. degree programme in Mining Engineering as a special case. In 2001, she joined CSIR as a project fellow and eventually became a Principal Scientist at the Central Institute of Mining and Fuel Research, Nagpur. She also holds a Ph.D. in Rock Mechanics and Numerical Modelling.

example_from_field.organization: Special Centre for Nanoscience (SCNS), Jawaharlal Nehru University (JNU), New Delhi
research_areas: Development of Nanobiochip for Oral Cancer Detection
example_from_field.details: Pratima R. Solanki works as Assistant Professor at the Special Centre for Nanoscience (SCNS), Jawaharlal Nehru University (JNU), New Delhi and is currently heading the Nanobiogroup at SCNS. Her group is working on development of nanobiochip for oral cancer detection, vitamins and microfluidic-based mycotoxin detection. The main research area is the integration of nanotechnology methods, tools and materials into various biosensor platforms for application in environmental and clinical analysis.

example_from_field.details: Joshua Berrier is an Optical Engineer at NASA Goddard Space Flight Center in Greenbelt, Maryland. He has served on more than 20 space flight projects including the James Webb Space Telescope, Roman Space Telescope and Parker Solar Probe. Currently, he is the AI&T lead for a space-qualified rover lidar that will be the first 3D imaging system of its kind designed to support wheeled vehicles on lunar, Martian and other extraterrestrial surfaces.

example_from_field.name: Sanchita Banerjee

example_from_field.details: Sanchita Banerjee is the first woman engineer of Oil India Ltd. An alumni of Calcutta University where she studied engineering. She joined Oil India Limited and started working as shift engineer at the Extraction plant of LPG from Natural Gas through cryogenic process in Assam. Within the organization, she has been instrumental in bringing about policies on maternity leave, child care leave and worked on issues of foreign training and foreign posting for women employees, management training for junior employees, and so on. She is now a retired executive director at Oil India Ltd.

steps.details: Complete 10+2 in any stream, preferably the Science stream (Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics).
Appear for the National Defence Academy (NDA) examination and the Service Selection Board (SSB) interview. All candidates must pass physical and medical tests.

Complete a Bachelor's degree in any Science stream and appear for the Combined Defence Services (CDS) Examination and Service Selection Board (SSB) interview. All candidates must pass physical and medical tests.

For technical entry, complete a Bachelor's degree (B.E./B.Tech.) in Engineering after clearing entrance examinations such as JEE Advanced, JEE Main, MET, VITEEE, MHT CET, SRMJEEE, COMEDK, UGET, KCET and others.
For entry into the Indian Armed Forces, candidates must clear various written examinations, an SSB interview and various medical examinations, after which they can join the Officers Training Academy.

example_from_field.details: Amy O'Connor currently serves as Chief Data and Information Officer at Precisely. scholarships.name: Kishore Vaigyanik Protsahan Yojana Scholarship
scholarships.details: This scholarship is provided to students enrolled in the first year of their engineering undergraduate course. Eligibility includes an aptitude test. It is awarded by the Government of India for economically backward students who have a strong aptitude for scientific research.
About 20,000 such engineering scholarships are awarded by the Ministry of Minority Affairs every year. The full course fees of awardees is directly paid to their institutions under this scheme.

The IOC Ltd offers 300 scholarships every year to eligible students. Students need to be enrolled in an undergraduate course. The scholarship particularly focuses on students with physical disabilities, women and students from North East and J&K.

scholarships.name: Kishore Vaigyanik Protsahan Yojana Scholarship
scholarships.details: This scholarship is provided to students enrolled in the first year of their engineering undergraduate course. Eligibility includes an aptitude test. It is awarded by the Government of India for economically backward students who have a strong aptitude for scientific research.
About 20,000 such engineering scholarships are awarded by the Ministry of Minority Affairs every year. The full course fees of awardees is directly paid to their institutions under this scheme.

The IOC Ltd offers 300 scholarships every year to eligible students. Students need to be enrolled in an undergraduate course. The scholarship particularly focuses on students with physical disabilities, women and students from North East and J&K.

example_from_field.details: Dr. Gangandeep Kang, an Indian Virologist, is hailed as the 'vaccine godmother' of India. example_from_field.details: Ms. Kalpana Maharana, a resident of Brundabanpur Gram Panchayat of Ranpur Block, Nayagarh district, is one such artisan who has established a successful AppliquÃ© works venture with the support of Odisha Livelihood Mission (OLM). On an average, she earns about Rs 11,000 to Rs 15,000 on a monthly basis by selling her products. This is a reality today due to the financial support she secured through Start-Up Village Entrepreneurship Program (SVEP) at a minimum interest rate. In the beginning, Kalpana was granted a loan of Rs 50,000 under the SVEP. After the successful repayment of the loan, she received Rs 60,000 from the Community Investment Fund (CIF) through the Brundaban Gram Panchayat Level Federation (GPLF). This helped her to employ more women in her business.

achievements: Learned carpentry by observing her father

example_from_field.details: Priti, as a young girl, would watch closely and learn from her father. She was very interested in carpentry and learned everything just by watching him, making her first cupboard at the age of 20, which she also managed to sell. Priti started her first workshop in 2014 and was the only female assistant carpenter there. Trained in business development under the Skill India Mission, she now dreams of opening a large showroom someday. Today, Priti is applying the skills she learnt, with her business steadily growing. Priti gets very annoyed at the gender discrimination still rampant at her workplace and is of the opinion that everyone is capable of any work. She also considers no work as too big or too small. To succeed, one should just follow their dreams and keep at it by working hard.

education.institution: Mayo School of Arts (National College of Arts, Lahore)

achievements: Mastered the art of Plaster of Paris and carpentry
Worked with renowned Sikh architectural masters Bhai Ram Singh and Sir Ganga Ram
Participated in the design of Imperial Forest Research Institute, Dehradun
Contributed to Indian Military Academy
Worked on Lalgarh Palace
Worked on Lakshmi Niwas Palace
Worked on Gajner Palace
Worked on Bejoy Bhawan (Veterinary University)
example_from_field.details: Ala Singh went to Mayo School of Arts (National College of Arts, Lahore) in 1883-1884 where he mastered the art of Plaster of Paris and carpentry from Bhai Ram Singh and Sir Ganga Ram, the veterans of the Sikh architectural school of thought. In 1890, he started working with his masters and other architects. Ala Singh assisted in the design of Imperial Forest Research Institute, Dehradun, ceiling plastering and woodwork at Indian Military Academy, Lalgarh Palace, Lakshmi Niwas Palace, Gajner Palace, and Bejoy Bhawan (Veterinary University). A century later, his grandson Swaranjit Singh found some testimonials to his brilliance from his old house near Manna Singh Chowk in the walled city of Amritsar.

example_from_field.details: Harjindar Singh is the elder son of a family with one sister and two brothers. After completion of his Class 12 examinations he had been looking for a job. He came to know of MITCON and the opportunity of training in the automotive sector followed by a job. He contacted the field staff of MITCON who made him aware of the skill development programme in two wheeler repairing being conducted. It was free of cost being conducted by the Ministry of MSME with MITCON as a partner institution. Harjindar underwent the training and successfully completed the course and received a completion certificate from the Ministry of MSME at the end of the course. He started working under the supervision of a Senior Service Manager at a Bajaj Service Station. After completing his apprenticeship he joined a Bajaj Service Station as Service Advisor and is now earning a salary of INR 8000. This enables him to provide economic support to his family.

example_from_field.details: Ankita Shah was hit by polio but never let challenges define her. After completing her graduation, she took up odd jobs to support her family. It was not easy to find a permanent position. She decided to learn how to drive an auto. With the help of her friend, she even got her auto-rickshaw customized with hand-operated brakes. This helped her to drive the auto quickly. With this intelligent decision, she doubled her income. 'Thanks to my rickshaw, I earn double the amount I was earning in my previous jobs. To increase my earnings further, I have joined a cab aggregator platform as well.' Ankita's decision of driving an auto-rickshaw has benefited her a lot. She earns Rs. 25,000 every month. Starting something on her own meant she could decide her own timings. So, along with working and earning money, Ankita is also able to take care of her family.

example_from_field.details: Pratibha Kumbhar, a 35-year-old mother of two, is making up for lost time. She worked in her husband's tailoring shop till she heard about the training in soldering and job opportunity in a fast-expanding electric vehicle company. She undertook the training and now she assembles circuits for EV speedometers at a factory in Pune - her first job as a formal worker with fixed wages. 'I work fixed hours and I am financially independent,' said Kumbhar, assembling circuits with pink-gloved fingers on an all-female shop-floor at Kinetic Communications, a manufacturer of EV components and a subsidiary of Indian auto-maker Kinetic Group.

careers.description: A Mat Weaver, Bamboo is trained in making mats of seasoned and coloured bamboo strips according to a given pattern or design. They are trained in pre-weaving processes such as soaking pre-cut bamboo stakes in water to soften and bleach them. They also learn how to remove stakes from bath when bamboo strips are soft, then cutting and removing splints and splitting them into strips of desired size and thickness. They also learn to colour strips according to desired pattern and weaving them into mats. They learn to cut off and conceal or weave together loose ends to finish edges. Finally they have to produce a finished mat using a loom.
personality_traits: You have the ability to sit and work for long hours
You like to create artistic things

example_from_field.name: Jessie Jayaraj
example_from_field.profession: Entrepreneur and Bamboo Utility Product Artisan
achievements: Promoted awareness about natural fibres as an alternative to plastic
Started from a small room with one tailor
Built a successful enterprise employing around 40 tailors
Produces bags, files and stationery products
Works with cotton, bamboo fibre and jute materials
Supplies promotional products to corporate companies
Expanded business through self-financing and hard work
Aims to secure export orders
example_from_field.details: Hailing from the small town of Kottayam in Kerala, Jessie Jayaraj did not have grand dreams of being an entrepreneur. But she set on a mission to spread awareness about natural fibres against plastic by promoting natural products. What started from a small room and with one tailor's help has now become an enterprise in Tondiarpet, where products like bags, files, and stationery products are stitched. As an alternative for synthetic products, she takes orders for products made of cotton, bamboo fibre, jute, to name a few. Corporate companies, which have a huge employee base, play an important role in spreading awareness against non-biodegradable products. They give away a lot of promotional products, which could be made using natural fibres. MNCs have become much more conscious about the use of natural fibres. Her unit now hires around 40 tailors who work with her six days a week. Speaking about her role, she said: 'I do everything from meeting clients to taking orders, working on design, then as a salesperson and delivering too.' Jessie believes making such products popular among masses is the best role she can play to spread awareness. It has taken long years to reach here and where she stands today is the result of sheer hard work. Jessie said the growth process was tough one, yet humbling. The mother of two built her venture with a very small investment, amidst several challenges on the personal and business fronts. Jessie is aiming at export orders now.

example_from_field.details: Gareeb Nawaj, a CCTV Installation Technician trainee at PMKK Empower Pragati, Bijnor, came from a family where several members depended on his father's income. example_from_field.name: Tarishi Jain
achievements: Founded Ajoobaa in 2018
Built a startup focused on handmade wool crochet and knitted wear for children up to four years
Started the business from home after creating handmade crochet items
Expanded operations to approximately 4,000 orders per month
Products have been used by at least 20,000 children
Created employment opportunities for 112 artisans, mostly homemakers
Achieved an annual turnover of approximately Rs. 1.4 crore
example_from_field.details: Entrepreneur Tarishi Jain always preferred handmade products. In 2015, while pregnant with her daughter, she knitted a beautiful frock which inspired her to return to knitting and crocheting after a long break. She began making TV covers, handkerchiefs and laces, and later created a handmade frock for her daughter in just two days. As demand for her handcrafted items grew among friends and acquaintances, she recognized an opportunity to support women working from home through knitting and crocheting. This led to the establishment of Ajoobaa in 2018. Based in Sonipat, Haryana, the startup sells handmade wool crochet and knitted wear for young children. Over time, the business grew substantially, handling around 4,000 orders every month and employing 112 artisans, primarily homemakers. The company has dressed over 20,000 children and achieved an annual turnover of around Rs. 1.4 crore.

careers.career_name: DTH Set Top Box Installation and Service Technician
careers.description: Direct-to-Home (DTH) Set-Top Box or Cable Operators are a part of our life nowadays. There are many service providers at the national level and even local level who employ DTH Set Top Box Installation & Service Technicians in their organisation. These technicians are trained in installing the set-top box at customers' premises. They connect the set-top box to the TV or home theatres. They are also trained in identifying the faults in the DTH system and carry out repairs. They are the link between the customers and the service providers. They also guide the customers for better service.

careers.description: E-Waste Collection is an important task required for the sustainability of the environment. It is the collection of old electronic products like refrigerators, TVs, computers and other electronic devices. The tasks an E-Waste Collector is expected to perform include not only collecting e-waste from retailers, repair shops and other unorganised stakeholders but disposing of these in an environmentally friendly process. They are responsible for promoting the importance of e-waste management in society and creating awareness about the ill effects of improper handling of e-waste. They are also responsible for packaging the e-waste, labelling and maintaining logs about transportation and finally transporting the collected e-waste to the central warehouse.

example_from_field.name: Sohaib Malik
achievements: Entered the organized e-waste sector in 2018
Expanded sustainable e-waste collection operations
Improved business conditions and profitability
Works with certified responsible recyclers
Sources e-waste from about 100 smaller dealers

example_from_field.details: When Karo Sambhav started promoting responsible recycling, many scrap merchants were initially hesitant. Through relationship building and awareness efforts, Sohaib Malik, a third-generation aggregator from Mustafabad, Delhi, entered the e-waste sector in 2018. His family had been involved in scrap trading for decades. By working with Karo Sambhav and certified recyclers, he expanded his operations sustainably. Workers no longer need to manually dismantle harmful electronic waste, and instead package and transport electronics safely for recycling. His business improved significantly, and he now sources e-waste from around 100 smaller dealers in New Delhi as well as from states such as Bihar and West Bengal.

example_from_field.details: Bharat Kalia took the entrepreneurial plunge by quitting his management consultancy job to launch Lifelong Online and manufacture mixer grinders. example_from_field.details: Shri Abhishek Trivedi, a resident of Lachhipur village in Raebareli District of Uttar Pradesh, completed Class 12 in 2014. Initially unemployed, he worked part-time in an internet parlour earning Rs. 2,000 per month. After learning about training and placement opportunities offered by Skill Development Institute (SDI), Raebareli, he enrolled in the Fitter trade. Following counselling, he underwent a three-month training programme in Pipe Fitter - City Gas Distribution. After successfully completing the training in February 2018, he was selected as Pipe Fitter by Sambhav Project Pvt. Ltd., working on behalf of Green Gas Limited to maintain gas pipelines in Kanpur city. He was offered a monthly salary of Rs. 12,000.

example_from_field.name: Raju Kannan
achievements: Built a successful manufacturing and exporting company
Started career as an ordinary fitter
Progressed to become an A-grade fitter
Worked as a Tool Room Helper in 1958 at TANSI
Later joined Sakthi Sugars Factory
Established Andavar Lathe Works with his sons
Serves approximately 32,000 customers
Exports products to 24+ countries
example_from_field.details: Founded by Mr. Raju Kannan, Andavar Lathe Works is a veteran company with 55 years of excellence in manufacturing and exporting oil mill machines in India. Mr. Raju Kannan started his career as an ordinary fitter and gradually progressed through the industry. Hailing from a farming family in Erode, he began his career as a Tool Room Helper in 1958 at TANSI, a Tamil Nadu government agency. Later, he worked as a helper at Sakthi Sugars Factory where he developed his skills and rose to the highest level as an A-grade fitter with extensive technical knowledge. He later established Andavar Lathe Works along with his sons, Mr. Bala Subramaniyam and Mr. Shanmuga Sundaram. Today, the company serves around 32,000 customers not only in India but also across 24 countries.

An important part of their job is to keep the customers happy at all times.

example_from_field.details: Jasim Shekhra came from a financially weak family of five members. His father, Mr. Anwarul, was the sole earning member while his mother was a housewife. After leaving his studies, Jasim worked as a daily wage labourer. Later, through the mobilization team of Don Bosco Tech Society's Purnea, Bihar centre, he enrolled in the Food & Beverage Service Steward training programme. After successful completion of the training, he was selected through campus placement by Barbeque Nation in Chennai. He earns Rs. 11,000 per month along with free food and accommodation. He continues his studies through the National Institute of Open Schooling (NIOS) while supporting his siblings in completing their education. His family's financial condition improved significantly due to his employment.

example_from_field.details: Faheem Sultana hails from Bapu Nagar, Serilingampally. She worked as a domestic helper in 3 to 4 households and earned between INR 2,000 and INR 3,000 per month in total. She underwent a PMKVY Training Program in General Housekeeper, where she learned cleaning, mopping, dusting, the use of cleaning agents through practical sessions, safety, hygiene and life skills. Though she attended the training while working, she found the program highly beneficial.

example_from_field.name: Amruta Kamath
achievements: Started her career as a home chef

example_from_field.details: Amruta Kamath's passion for cooking inspired her to begin as a home chef. She operated a home kitchen for two years, serving home-cooked meals to friends and acquaintances. As demand and appreciation for her food grew, she continuously reinvented dishes and eventually specialized in Asian and Continental cuisine. During the lockdown, she attended a cloud kitchen course to better understand food business operations. After learning the essentials of the trade, she rented a small kitchen space, hired three sous chefs and launched House of Flavours. Today, House of Flavours delivers food between Borivali and Andheri and also accepts bulk orders across Mumbai.

example_from_field.details: Evanisha, from Umjarasi village of Nongpoh in Ri-Bhoi District of Meghalaya, faced family difficulties after the passing of her father in 2014. A friend informed her about the skill training program conducted by Don Bosco Tech Society. She visited the Rural Resource and Training Centre (RRTC) in Shillong and, after counselling, enrolled in the Housekeeping Manual Cleaning Attendant Service program. She completed a 40-day training course that included housekeeping skills, dance, theatrical activities and life skills development. The program helped her become more confident and prepared for employment. She successfully cleared interviews and joined WeWork TCFM in Bengaluru as a Housekeeping Trainee, earning a stipend of INR 12,000 along with free food and accommodation.

example_from_field.details: Navendu Mishra, who was working in the social sector, always wanted to start something on his own. Coming from the agriculture belt of India, he was fascinated by the idea of milling his own oil. This led to the beginning of Shree Ghani. Initially, he sought support from another local proprietor and started milling coconut, sesame seeds, groundnut and mustard seeds. Within two years, he established a strong customer base and successfully expanded his business.
example_from_field.source: Personal interview with Mr. Navendu Mishra

example_from_field.name: Krishnendu Datta
example_from_field.education: Graduate from National Institute of Fashion Technology (NIFT)
achievements: Built a business exporting jute products internationally
Exports jute products to countries such as New Zealand and Australia
Promoted jute as a lifestyle and fashion product
Benefited from the growing demand for eco-friendly products
Contributed to the expansion of India's jute export market
industry_insights: A quality leather bag costs around Rs. 800, while an equally fashionable jute bag costs around Rs. 250
Jute product exports were expected to increase by 200% over two years
Exports of diversified jute products have increased by more than 150% since 1999
The jute sector provides employment to nearly two lakh artisans
Around 12,000 jute units operate across India
example_from_field.details: Jute, once used mainly as a raw material for gunny sacks, has emerged as a major eco-friendly lifestyle and fashion product in both domestic and international markets. Increased profit margins and growing demand have encouraged entrepreneurs such as Krishnendu Datta. A graduate from the National Institute of Fashion Technology (NIFT), Datta exports jute products to countries including New Zealand and Australia. The rapid growth of diversified jute products and exports has helped create employment opportunities for artisans and expanded the reach of the jute industry.

achievements: Registered as a home chef on Shero Home Food platform

training_and_support: Kitchen hygiene inspection through video calls

example_from_field.details: Shero Home Food has registered home chefs and provides catering services through delivery partners such as Swiggy, Zomato and Dunzo. Ayesha Maryam, a resident of Purasawalkam in Chennai and a mother of three, joined the platform after looking for work-from-home opportunities. After completing platform verification and training, she started accepting food orders from home. The platform trains home chefs in hygiene protocols, FSSAI compliance, inventory management, packing techniques, social media skills and order management. Ayesha successfully manages household responsibilities while operating her home-based cooking business.
